title: pilosella cymosa subsp. heldreichiana
intro: Pilosella cymosa subsp. heldreichiana, also known as the Heldreich's mouse-ear hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pilosella cymosa subsp. heldreichiana are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Pilosella cymosa subsp. heldreichiana include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
